title: Lesson Quiz: The Judicial Branch — Courts, interpretation of law, and judicial review.
description: Assessment of the concept that Courts, interpretation of law, and judicial review.

questions.prompt: What is the main role of the judicial branch?

choices.feedback: Incorrect. Making laws is the role of the legislative branch.
Incorrect. Enforcing laws is the role of the executive branch.
Correct. The judicial branch interprets laws and applies them to cases.
Incorrect. That is part of the executive branch.

questions.prompt: The Supreme Court is the highest court in the United States.

choices.feedback: Correct. The Supreme Court is the highest court.
Incorrect. The Supreme Court is the highest court.

questions.prompt: What does judicial review allow courts to do?

choices.feedback: Incorrect. Courts do not create laws.

choices.text: Decide if a law follows the Constitution
choices.feedback: Correct. Judicial review allows courts to determine constitutionality.
Incorrect. That is the role of the executive branch.
Incorrect. Representation is part of the legislative branch.

questions.prompt: Courts make laws when they interpret them.

choices.feedback: Incorrect. Courts interpret laws but do not make them.
Correct. Lawmaking is the role of the legislative branch.

questions.prompt: Which situation best represents the judicial branch in action?

choices.text: Congress votes on a new law
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That is the legislative branch.
Incorrect. That is the executive branch.

choices.text: A court reviews a law and decides if it is constitutional
choices.feedback: Correct. This is an example of judicial review by the judicial branch.
Incorrect. Voting is part of civic participation, not the judicial branch.
